sanskrit:
यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः।
इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः॥
transliteration: yatato hy api kaunteya puruṣasya vipaścitaḥ | indriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Even of the discriminating person who strives, O son-of-Kuntī, the turbulent senses violently snatch away the mind.
contextual_meaning: No matter how insightful or disciplined one is, raw sensory impulses can hijack executive control, illustrating the limits of will-power without higher absorption.
psychological_state: Ego-depletion: the moment disciplined resolve collapses under involuntary craving, exposing the need for a trans-cognitive anchor beyond sheer self-control.
trigger_scenario: A dieter who has studied nutrition still binges on cookies the night before a weigh-in.
anchor_text: Senses hijack even the wise; anchor in higher focus.
